title: Define MeshCore-to-LICHEN compatibility contract and mapping table
description: Why this exists: MeshCore app compatibility needs a clear local adapter contract that preserves LICHEN RF/mesh behavior. What needs to be done: define supported MeshCore local operations and map them to LICHEN HAL identity, local CoAP/status/config/message/key flows, or explicit unsupported errors. Include command semantics, response/error policy, transport assumptions, and state ownership. Acceptance criteria: mapping table is recorded in Beads notes or design field; RF protocol boundary is explicit; config/status/message flows have deterministic behavior; unsupported features and semantic mismatches are named.
design:
MeshCore-to-LICHEN compatibility contract, recorded 2026-07-01.

Scope and boundary:
- This is a local app compatibility contract only. It MUST NOT emit MeshCore RF packets, accept raw MeshCore packets for LoRa TX, or imply OTA interoperability with MeshCore nodes. LICHEN remains IPv6/SCHC/RPL/CoAP over the LICHEN link layer.
- MeshCore local clients speak a byte-command companion API over BLE NUS raw values or serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e length frames. The MeshCore adapter owns only this local transport/session/protocol surface.
- The adapter maps supported app operations to LICHEN HAL identity/capability/status, local app message ingress/egress, and compatibility-local state. Unsupported or semantically unsafe operations return deterministic MeshCore ERR responses.

Existing LICHEN surfaces to use:
- HAL facts: lichen/subsys/lichen/hal/include/lichen/hal.h and hal.c expose board_name, zephyr_board, capabilities, radio model, UI profile, location/time provider, and per-capability status. Use these for DEVICE_INFO/SELF_INFO capability flags and battery/GNSS/radio availability placeholders.
- Gateway CoAP status/config: lichen/apps/gateway/src/main.c exposes /status, /neighbors, and /config. /config currently owns only tx_power_dbm. MeshCore config writes outside this surface are compatibility-state writes or unsupported until policy Beads define persistence.
- App-visible inbound events: lichen/apps/gateway/src/inbound_events.h/c defines generic text/status events but currently fans out only to Meshtastic when CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_ADAPTER is enabled. MeshCore should reuse or generalize this event boundary instead of adding a separate RF path.
- Meshtastic adapter pattern: lichen/apps/gateway/src/meshtastic_adapter.c and lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/include/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.h provide the local-session adapter pattern: bounded queues, adapter callbacks, HAL-derived local info, unsupported-operation catalog, and explicit app-compat non-RF boundary. MeshCore should follow this architecture with a MeshCore-specific codec/dispatcher.
- Native LICHEN: lichen/lib/native/include/lichen/native.h is the native CBOR local protocol and is not the MeshCore wire format. It is a semantic reference only; MeshCore must not share its frame marker or CBOR schema.

State ownership:
- HAL owns board/capability availability and device readiness.
- LICHEN L2/network/routing owns all RF packet send/receive, routing, and protocol security.
- Gateway local message/event layer owns app-facing ingress/egress events once a concrete local message contract exists.
- MeshCore adapter owns MeshCore command parsing, response encoding, command serialization expectations, connected-session queues, offline-message compatibility queue, and MeshCore-local compatibility state.
- MeshCore identity projection is not owned by HAL today. It is blocked on project-LICHEN-9c4y.14 and must define the 32-byte public key plus 6-byte prefix before direct contacts/private messages are treated as supported.
- MeshCore channel/config secret handling is blocked on project-LICHEN-9c4y.17. Native LICHEN group/OSCORE/key material MUST NOT be exposed as MeshCore channel secrets.

Response/error policy:
- Parse errors, malformed frames, bad lengths, bad indexes, and out-of-range values return MeshCore RESP_CODE_ERR with ERR_CODE_ILLEGAL_ARG where the command has a response path.
- Unsupported commands return RESP_CODE_ERR with ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD.
- Missing peer/channel/contact entries return ERR_CODE_NOT_FOUND.
- Full queues or exhausted compatibility tables return ERR_CODE_TABLE_FULL.
- Temporarily invalid state, such as command requiring an active session or busy iterator, returns ERR_CODE_BAD_STATE.
- Commands accepted as read-only compatibility no-ops must be explicitly listed by the command matrix; they may return OK only when returning OK will not mislead the app into believing an RF/secret/radio mutation occurred.
- The dispatcher must never silently drop a recognized request. Async push frames may be dropped only according to a documented bounded queue/drop policy.

Mapping table:

| MeshCore command/flow | LICHEN mapping | Contract status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BLE NUS UUIDs / raw values | MeshCore transport surface; requires CONFIG_BT peripheral and HAL BLE-local policy | Supported when project-LICHEN-9c4y.4/9c4y.15 implement it |
| Serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e length frames | MeshCore transport surface over HAL serial-local or optional TCP | Optional; serial should use HAL serial-local, TCP only if explicitly enabled |
| CMD_APP_START -> SELF_INFO | Build from HAL identity/caps, uptime, MeshCore compatibility identity projection, radio/GNSS flags | Mandatory; identity fields blocked on 9c4y.14 |
| CMD_DEVICE_QUERY -> DEVICE_INFO | Build from compatibility protocol version, max contacts/channels/queue limits, board/model/version strings, BLE PIN policy | Mandatory; BLE PIN/security details blocked on 9c4y.15 |
| CMD_GET_CONTACTS | Return empty list until peer/contact projection exists; later synthesize from LICHEN peer/contact table | Mandatory safe baseline, richer behavior blocked on 9c4y.14 |
| CMD_GET_CONTACT_BY_KEY | Resolve by compatibility public key/prefix once projection exists | Unsupported/not-found until 9c4y.14 and peer table exist |
| Contact add/update/remove/share/export/import/reset path | Compatibility-local contact state only; must not mutate LICHEN trust pins without explicit key-management design | Optional or unsupported by default |
| CMD_GET_CHANNEL | Return deterministic compatibility channel slots, names, and compatibility-local secret/placeholder policy | Mandatory baseline, policy blocked on 9c4y.17 |
| CMD_SET_CHANNEL / delete channel | Persist only MeshCore-local compatibility config if policy allows; never write native group/OSCORE secrets directly | Optional; blocked on 9c4y.17 |
| CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG | Validate text type/channel/length, then call LICHEN local message ingress as group/channel text when that contract exists | Mandatory if messaging is claimed; ack behavior drift blocked on 9c4y.16 and send API on 9c4y.7 |
| CMD_SEND_TXT_MSG direct/private | Map 6-byte prefix/public key to LICHEN peer destination after identity projection; otherwise deterministic unsupported/not-found | Optional; blocked on 9c4y.14 and 9c4y.7 |
| CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_DATA | Treat as optional binary app datagram. Only map if LICHEN app data contract defines data_type/path semantics | Optional; command drift covered by 9c4y.16 |
| Incoming message queue / MSG_WAITING / CMD_SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE | MeshCore adapter queues app-visible events from gateway inbound event boundary; client drains with SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE until NO_MORE_MESSAGES | Mandatory if incoming messaging is claimed; implementation in 9c4y.8 |
| CMD_GET_BATT_AND_STORAGE | Build from HAL battery/external-flash capabilities and status; return explicit unavailable placeholders where no provider exists | Mandatory read-only/status |
| CMD_GET_DEVICE_TIME / CMD_SET_DEVICE_TIME | GET from system time/uptime provider if available; SET may update compatibility-local time or return unsupported until a real time-set provider exists | GET mandatory placeholder; SET optional/read-only policy |
| CMD_SET_ADVERT_NAME / CMD_SET_ADVERT_LATLON | Compatibility-local app display fields only unless tied to native config/location policy later | Optional read/write compatibility state or unsupported |
| Radio params, TX power, tuning, other params | Map only tx_power_dbm to existing /config policy if safe; all RF modem/profile mutations that conflict with LICHEN are unsupported | Mostly unsupported/read-only; exact matrix in 9c4y.13/9c4y.17 |
| Stats, telemetry, auto-add, default flood scope, path hash mode | Return read-only placeholders or compatibility-local values where harmless | Optional/stubbed |
| Repeater/admin/login/status/binary/anon/path discovery/trace/control/raw data/raw packet/KISS/private-key/signing/factory-reset/reboot | No safe LICHEN local mapping; must return deterministic ERR or disabled response | Unsupported for MVP |

Semantic mismatches to keep explicit:
- MeshCore app identities are 32-byte public keys plus 6-byte prefixes; LICHEN native identities are IPv6/IID plus LICHEN key/trust state. Do not use unstable hashes without collision and rotation policy.
- MeshCore channel secrets are a local app concept and must not expose LICHEN OSCORE/group keying material.
- MeshCore path/flood/repeater semantics do not map to LICHEN RPL/IPv6 routing. App-visible path fields may be placeholders but cannot drive RF forwarding.
- MeshCore command flow has no sequence numbers. The adapter must serialize request handling per session and interleave async push frames safely.
- Real MeshCore app behavior may differ by client version. project-LICHEN-9c4y.16 handles command drift; project-LICHEN-9c4y.11 handles real-client smoke testing.

Downstream requirements:
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.3 should choose a handwritten bounded C codec/dispatcher strategy rather than protobuf or CBOR generation; the protocol is fixed byte fields and little-endian integers.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.5 should implement bounded frame validation, per-session command dispatch, output queueing, and the error policy above.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.6 should implement DEVICE_INFO/SELF_INFO/channel/contact/status read handlers using HAL and compatibility state.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.7/9c4y.8 should define the concrete local message ingress/egress bridge shared with the gateway event boundary.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.9 should consume the unsupported table above and the detailed matrix from 9c4y.13.
notes: Review evidence: two independent review subagents completed after the design field and follow-up Beads were recorded. Mill the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 blockers and confirmed consistency with HAL, gateway /status,/neighbors,/config, inbound_events, Meshtastic adapter, native protocol, and MeshCore research; residual risks are tracked by project-LICHEN-gbx6, project-LICHEN-9c4y.14, .17, .16, and .11. Hubble the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 blockers and confirmed the mapping table, RF boundary, deterministic response/error policy, unsupported features, semantic mismatches, and follow-up coverage satisfy acceptance.
close_reason: Completed: compatibility contract and mapping table recorded in design field, gaps routed to follow-up Beads, and two independent reviews found no closure blockers.
